title: Thread Review: CodeSync workflow control adoption path
review_status_summary: The thread is usable, but PM review must close proof or handoff gaps before it should advance.

stage_rail.title: Research Synthesis
stage_rail.summary: The research pack was condensed into a first-pass research notebook and research brief.

stage_rail.title: Prd Handoff
stage_rail.summary: A first-pass PRD was generated from the adopted notes and research pack, but it still requires PM review before handoff is treated as strong.

sections.title: Market and competitor context
sections.headline: What the adopted research says about wedge strength and proof gaps.
sections.summary: The adopted research says the wedge is promising, but proof and positioning still need validation.
key_points: A narrow workflow-control launch lane is stronger than a broad platform narrative.
Security and ROI claims still need explicit proof.

stage_states.summary: The research pack was condensed into a first-pass research notebook and research brief.

sections.title: PRD
sections.headline: The adopted PRD is present, but still provisional.
sections.summary: The adopted workspace should make CodeSync reviewable as a governed workflow-control product with one explicit launch lane and a visible review queue for unresolved proof gaps.
key_points: The adopted PRD is a first-pass artifact.
Delivery planning should wait until review closes major proof gaps.

stage_states.summary: A first-pass PRD was generated from the adopted notes and research pack, but it still requires PM review before handoff is treated as strong.

action_items.title: Confirm the first launch lane and keep the adopted PRD scoped to it.
action_items.rationale: Use the narrow workflow-control lane as the first bounded external story instead of broadening to a full platform claim.

action_items.title: Add prototype proof before treating the thread as handoff-ready.
action_items.rationale: Prototype proof remains a required step before the adopted PRD should be treated as ready for downstream delivery planning.

pending_questions: Which claims are supported strongly enough for external reuse?
What prototype proof is still required?
key_risks: Validate pricing, packaging, and launch-lane packaging assumptions.
decisions: Treat the adopted PRD as provisional until PM review resolves proof gaps.
pinned_context.confidence_summary: The adopted problem, concept, segment, persona, and PRD artifacts are usable as a first pass, but customer claims, security posture, and packaging still require explicit PM review.

recommended_next_step: Confirm the first launch lane and keep the adopted PRD scoped to it.
